evidence_strength: committed configuration, routing code, and deterministic contract tests
finding: Cloudflare Pages cannot create and deploy a Durable Object inside the Pages project. The Durable Object must be deployed as a separate Worker and externally bound into the Pages Function.

verification.scope: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source created for commit

privacy_boundary: Only a SHA-256 reservation key hash and bounded request metadata cross the Pages-to-Durable-Object boundary. No raw private chat, health data, credentials, or identifying personal data are introduced.
limits: No Cloudflare deployment was performed because account credentials and environment configuration remain unavailable.
Static validation does not prove the external binding exists in the Cloudflare account.
No deployed concurrency, persistence, hostname, or served-commit proof exists yet.
The nodejs_compat flag is required because the reused reservation module imports node:crypto.
falsification_route: Deploy the Durable Object Worker and Pages project with the external binding.
Send concurrent requests with the same reservation_key_hash and verify exactly one 201 and all later responses 409.
Redeploy and verify the reservation persists.
Capture the Worker and Pages deployment identities and served commit.
